mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% triggers a labor market signal that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 7.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 700,000 units triggers a supply signal that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 700,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in industrial production and housing starts signals a weakening demand cycle that compresses equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with joint decline in INDPRO and HOUST are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary signal that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial unemployment claims below 250,000 triggers a labor market signal that predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 250,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in real GDP and industrial production signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with joint increase in GDPC1 and INDPRO are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
